--- SOURCE 1: Why prevention and vaccination in animal health matter ---
URL: https://www.boehringer-ingelheim.com/animal-health/trends-insights/why-prevention-and-vaccination-animal-health-matter

SUMMARY:
Livestock: Farmers vaccinate not just to save individual animals but to protect entire herds or flocks. Healthy livestock means increased feed intake, better production of eggs, milk, meat and other goods . A disease outbreak can wipe out years of hard work and investment, but vaccines help reduce that risk.

--- SOURCE 2: Vaccines - HealthforAnimals - Health for Animals ---
URL: https://healthforanimals.org/animal-medicines/vaccines/

SUMMARY:
Vaccines are one of the most important tools available to care for animal health and, by extension, our own health. Livestock vaccination reduces GHG emissions.

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://healthforanimals.org/wp-content/themes/healthforanimals/assets/images/logo.png)
![](https://healthforanimals.org/wp-content/themes/healthforanimals/assets/images/logo.png)

Importance of Animals

We share our world with billions of animals. Many of them are raised on farms while others share our home as pets. They support economies, improve diets, provide companionship and more.
  
Explore the pages to the right and learn why our world relies on healthy animals.

Global Challenges

Our planet is rapidly changing. The population is growing; more people are joining the middle class. All of them deserve good nutrition and health.
  
Explore the pages to the right and see how veterinarians working alongside farmers, researchers, and others can help us meet this challenge.

Resources

Sign up for our Newsletter to for original articles, interviews, and infographics each month.
  
Read our Antibiotics Commitment to see how we support responsible use, or view the Antibiotics FAQ to learn exactly how these medicines are used.
  
Visit the Story of Animal Health or Animal Health Matters to discover the value of health across the globe.

**Animal health in Data**  
  
View our collection of animal health data in areas such as: antimicrobial resistance, sustainability, disease, parasites, and more. All data is freely available for use with citation.

[Read more](https://healthforanimals.org/animal-health-in-data/)

![](/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/data-thumbnail.png)

## Vaccines

#### Disease prevention through vaccination is essential in helping animals live healthy lives.

The benefits of vaccines have long been understood by generations of parents who’ve protected the health of their children. Similarly, for farmers, veterinarians and pet owners, vaccines provide essential protections against illness in livestock and pets.

Vaccination offers an effective, inexpensive way to prevent a deadly disease from harming the animals in their care. These medicines are foundational to any animal health program, with the benefits extending even to public health.

When animals are protected against a zoonotic disease, it prevents transfer to people. This is especially important for a disease like rabies which still kills nearly 60,000 people every year in developing regions.

Vaccines are one of the most important tools available to care for animal health and, by extension, our own health.

### How to Increase Animal Vaccination

### More

## Quick facts

#### Rinderpest devasted cattle across the world for decades. Through vaccines and a globally coordinated effort, the disease was declared [successfully eradicated](http://www.oie.int/for-the-media/rinderpest/) in 2011.

#### A [study found](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14997239/ "WHO Study found") that every $1 spent on vaccinating livestock against Brucellosis provided $3.20 in benefits to society

#### Globally, on average, every two cattle vaccinated [is correlated with](https://healthforanimals.org/resources/publications/publications/animal-health-and-sustainability-a-global-data-analysis-summary/ " is correlated with") one person avoiding hunger

#### A 40% vaccination rate in Brazil [is associated with](https://healthforanimals.org/resources/publications/publications/animal-health-and-sustainability-a-global-data-analysis-summary/ "is associated with") a 12.8% reduction in land use for livestock

HealthforAnimals represents the animal health sector: manufacturers of veterinary pharmaceuticals, vaccines and other animal health products throughout the world, as well as the associations that represent companies at national and regional levels (referred to as Members).

# Preventative Health for Livestock: Vaccination Schedules and Parasite Control

![A veterinarian in a blue uniform prepares to give a vaccination to a white and brown cat. The cat sits calmly on a white blanket, looking away from the syringe. The room is bright with natural light.](https://mid-valleyvet.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/pet-health-vaccination-cat.jpg)

## The Importance of Preventative Care in Livestock Management

Preventative health care is critical to ensuring **livestock productivity, welfare, and long-term profitability**. Diseases can result in:

A structured herd health program focusing on **vaccinations and parasite control** helps mitigate these risks, ensuring **optimal herd performance and economic stability**.

### Vaccination Strategies for Livestock

Vaccination is a cornerstone of livestock disease prevention, **protecting against common infectious diseases** and reducing **the need for antibiotic treatments**. However, **vaccine effectiveness depends on proper administration, timing, and herd management practices**.

#### Common Causes of Vaccine Failure

Despite following standard vaccination protocols, **vaccine failure** can occur due to:

Understanding these issues can **maximize vaccine efficacy** and prevent outbreaks. Learn more about [Causes of Vaccine Failure in Beef Cattle](https://extension.psu.edu/causes-of-vaccine-failure-in-beef-cattle).

For detailed vaccination strategies, refer to [Vaccination Strategies to Maximize Preventive Health](https://www.vet.cornell.edu/animal-health-diagnostic-center/programs/nyschap/modules-documents/vaccination-strategies-maximize-preventive-health-and-minimize-adverse-effects-market-quality).

#### Vaccination Considerations for Small Ruminants and Swine

Sheep, goats, and pigs also require **species-specific vaccination programs** to prevent diseases such as:

For a detailed guide, read [The General Practitioner’s Guide to Small Ruminants and Camelids](https://todaysveterinarypractice.com/preventive-medicine/the-general-practitioners-guide-to-small-ruminants-and-camelids/).

### Parasite Control Strategies for Livestock

Parasitic infections significantly impact **weight gain, feed efficiency, fertility, and immune function**. Both **internal and external parasites** threaten livestock health and require **comprehensive parasite management plans**.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Vaccination strengthens the immune system, protecting livestock from infectious diseases, thus enhancing overall animal health and welfare.
Vaccines effectively prevent outbreaks of common diseases, reducing morbidity and mortality rates in livestock, thereby safeguarding animal populations.
Healthy vaccinated animals exhibit increased productivity, including higher yields of milk, meat, and eggs, contributing to better economic outcomes for farmers.
By maintaining animal health through vaccination, food production remains stable, ensuring a consistent supply of safe food for growing populations.
Investing in livestock vaccination helps prevent disease outbreaks, minimizing economic losses and promoting a stable agricultural economy.
